general: Always keep digital and physical copies of important documents (passport, insurance, bookings).
Notify your bank about your travel dates to avoid card blocks abroad.
Register with your country's embassy if traveling to a high-risk area.
Learn a few basic phrases in the local language -- locals appreciate the effort.
Keep emergency contacts written down, not just saved on your phone.
Take a photo of your luggage contents in case you need to file a lost baggage claim.
Arrive at the airport at least 2 hours before domestic and 3 hours before international flights.
Always carry a small first aid kit with basics: plasters, painkillers, antiseptic.
Check your phone plan for roaming charges or buy a local SIM / eSIM.
Share your itinerary with a trusted friend or family member.

beach: Reapply sunscreen every 2 hours, especially after swimming.
Stay hydrated -- heat and sun dehydrate you faster than you realize.
Watch out for strong currents. Always swim near lifeguard stations.
Wear reef-safe sunscreen to protect marine ecosystems.
Keep valuables locked up or in a waterproof pouch when at the beach.
city: Wear your bag in front of you in crowded areas to deter pickpockets.
Use official taxis or ride-share apps rather than unmarked vehicles.
Research free walking tours -- they're a great way to learn about a city.
Many museums offer free or discounted entry on certain days.
Keep a city map offline on your phone in case you lose signal.
mountain: Acclimatize gradually to higher altitudes to avoid altitude sickness.
Always tell someone your hiking route and expected return time.
Bring more water than you think you need.
Weather in mountains changes rapidly -- carry layers and rain protection.
Stick to marked trails and carry a physical map as backup.
cold_climate: Dress in layers: base layer (moisture-wicking), mid layer (insulation), outer layer (wind/waterproof).
Protect extremities -- most heat is lost from head, hands, and feet.
Keep electronics close to your body; cold drains batteries fast.
Eat calorie-rich foods to maintain body heat.
Watch for signs of frostbite: numbness, white/grayish skin patches.
tropical: Use DEET-based insect repellent to protect against mosquito-borne diseases.
Drink only bottled or purified water.
Be cautious with street food -- eat at busy stalls where food turnover is high.
Wear light, long-sleeved clothing in the evenings to avoid mosquito bites.
Check for required or recommended vaccinations well in advance.
countryside: Respect local wildlife and do not feed wild animals.
Close gates behind you when walking through farmland.
Check weather forecasts before heading out for long walks.
Bring cash as rural areas may have limited card acceptance.
Be aware of hunting seasons if exploring forests.

plane: Stay hydrated on flights -- cabin air is very dry.
Move around every 2 hours on long flights to prevent deep vein thrombosis (DVT).
Pack a change of clothes in your carry-on in case checked luggage is delayed.
Arrive early to allow time for security and unexpected delays.
Check airline baggage weight and size limits before packing.
car: Plan rest stops every 2 hours to avoid driver fatigue.
Check tire pressure, oil, and coolant before a long drive.
Research toll roads and have cash or a toll device ready.
Download offline maps for areas with poor cell coverage.
Carry a basic roadside emergency kit.
train: Book window seats for scenic routes.
Keep valuables close when sleeping on overnight trains.
Validate tickets before boarding where required (common in Europe).
Bring your own food and water for longer journeys.
Check if your rail pass covers seat reservations or if they're separate.
bus: Bring entertainment -- bus journeys can be long and repetitive.
Take motion sickness tablets before boarding if you're prone to travel sickness.
Keep valuables in a small bag on your lap, not in overhead storage.
Wear comfortable layers as bus temperatures can vary.
boat: Take motion sickness medication 30 minutes before boarding.
Focus on the horizon if you feel seasick.
Secure loose items in your cabin -- boats move a lot.
Apply sunscreen generously when on deck -- water reflects UV rays.

leisure: Don't over-plan -- leave room for spontaneous discoveries.
Set a daily budget to avoid overspending on holiday.
Try local restaurants rather than tourist-trap eateries.
Take a rest day if you're traveling for more than a week.
business: Research local business etiquette (greeting customs, gift-giving, punctuality).
Bring a universal power adapter for your devices.
Keep a portable steamer or iron to keep clothes crisp.
Schedule meetings accounting for jet lag -- give yourself a buffer day.
adventure: Always check weather conditions before outdoor activities.
Book reputable guides and operators for extreme activities.
Make sure your travel insurance covers adventure sports.
Start with easier trails/activities and build up to challenging ones.
cultural: Research dress codes for religious sites before visiting.
Ask permission before photographing locals or sacred sites.
Support local artisans by buying handmade souvenirs.
Hire local guides for deeper cultural insights.
romantic: Book at least one special restaurant in advance.
Plan a mix of activities and relaxation time together.
Surprise your partner with a small, thoughtful gesture during the trip.
Disconnect from screens and enjoy quality time.
